title: S. cerevisiae Δpho84 complementation: metal toxicity
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1df3r","text":"Testing for metal toxicity as a result of addition of a putative phosphate transporter to a S. cerevisiae Δpho84 strain","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":92,"length":13},{"style":"italic","offset":107,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Grow 10 ml S. cerevisiae strains BY5676 p416 GPD, BY2959 p416 GPD and BY2959 pXXX [p416 GPD + gene of interest] in Sc-ura medium for 16 hours at 30°C, 200 rpm shaking. Grow each strain in triplicate (3 independently obtained transformants).
Centrifuge cells at 1,810 x g for 3 minutes, wash twice and re-suspend in water.
Dilute cells to an OD 600 of 1.0 and prepare a dilution series (down to 10 -4 ).

Spot 10 µl of each dilution onto Sc-ura plates containing each metal to be assayed. Use a Sc-ura plate with no added metal as a positive control to ensure that all strains grow equally well Metals to try (try concentrations either side of these suggested starting points): %s %s Metal Suggested starting concentration Potassium chromate 300 µM Cobalt chloride 1 mM Zinc chloride 10 mM Sodium arsenate 3 mM Manganese chloride 10 mM Copper chloride 1 mM

Incubate plates for 72 hours at 30°C before imaging on a G:BOX imaging system (Syngene) to identify compounds on which toxicity was restored by the addition of pXXX. Use these results to optimise compound concentrations for a second run.
creator: David S Milner
